result:
Build is green (warnings only, no errors). No temporary instrumentation was added that needs removal.

**Root cause:** `crates/lua-stdlib/src/os_lib.rs:520` — the `os_date` strftime loop matches a conversion specifier via `check_strftime_option` but then emits nothing for every specifier (Phase-A stub). The failing assertion `assert(os.date(string.rep("%", 200)) == string.rep("%", 100))` (combined-line 802) needs `%%` to expand to a literal `%`; with the stub it expanded to nothing, so `""` ≠ `"%%..."` and the assertion fired.

**Fix:** Added a real, data-independent branch in `os_date` for the `%%` specifier — when `oplen == 1 && cc[1] == b'%'`, push one `b'%'` into the result buffer. Other specifiers (which depend on TmFields not yet derived from `t`) remain TODO(port). This is the actual strftime semantics for `%%`, not a workaround.

**New state:** I did not re-run the test (per the instructions — the mega-loop re-tests). Build passes (`cargo build -p lua-cli` clean of errors). The previously-failing assertion at combined-line 802 should now pass; the next likely failure shifts forward to combined-line ~807 (`load(os.date([[assert(D.year==%Y ...)]], t))()`), which requires real `%Y/%m/%d/...` expansion and is a separate Phase-B work item.

tool_input.command: cargo build -q -p lua-cli 2>&1 | grep -E "^(error|warning: unused|warning: unreachable)" | head -20; echo "EXIT $?"
